evidence: NFR-GCL targets node-local frequency routing missing in existing GCL.
Main evidence opportunity is heterophily, where local baselines show large method spread.
Builds on high/low-pass heterophily filtering but moves routing to node level.
Uses AF-GCL frequency-bias diagnosis as motivation.
Targets missing distinction between edge compatibility and edge existence.
Targets heterophily-aware hard negative safeguards.
Uses BGRL bootstrapping as base route.
Standalone edge calibration is close to adaptive augmentation and is killed as mainline.
Conditional residual InfoNCE samples same-difficulty negatives to avoid easy or unsafe graph negatives.
SRLP-GCL is anchored on heterophilous node classification where local baselines still have large spread.
The first pilot can be implemented as a narrow modification of an existing PyTorch GCL baseline.
The core target is a residual latent that removes the part ordinary message passing can already smooth in.
Reuses BGRL-style EMA target prediction while changing the target from augmented views to residual latent targets.
Builds from graph JEPA's context-target latent prediction but moves to node-level residual targets.
Uses latent prediction as a warning and contrast point; SRLP-GCL avoids full latent reconstruction.
Contrasts against generic graph joint embedding predictive SSL.
Motivates removing message-passing shortcuts from positive targets.
Used as a boundary condition: avoid claiming node-wise spectral routing novelty.
HEATS is the closest global positive sampling prior; DCGCL differs by dual-teacher disagreement calibration.
SPGCL motivates avoiding trivial positive pre-alignment under message passing.
HLCL motivates heterophily-aware handling but DCGCL avoids frequency/filter routing.
ASPECT is a boundary condition against node-level spectral routing novelty claims.
DCGCL replaces residual latent prediction after SRLP main-method claim failed.
Separates feature/topology compatibility from edge existence via dual teachers.
Uses soft prototype weights and disagreement to reduce unsafe positives and negatives.
Anchored on heterophilous node classification with Geom-GCN fixed splits.
DCA becomes the current empirical lead after prototype/route/filter training gates failed.
ASPECT is the closest node-level adaptive spectral fusion prior; DCA cannot claim broad spectral-fusion novelty.
SPGCL constrains positive-sample and message-passing shortcut claims.
Deferred anchors avoid forcing unsafe alignment between graph embeddings and filter coordinates during training.
Current evidence is centered on node classification with Chameleon official splits plus raw-dominance diagnostics for Texas/Wisconsin.
